mission.title: Install and Configure MetalLB (Bare-metal Load Balancer)
mission.description: MetalLB is a load-balancer implementation for bare metal Kubernetes clusters, utilizing standard routing protocols. It is ideal for environments where cloud load balancers are not available, providing a way to expose services externally. Key trade-offs include the need for proper network configuration and potential complexity in managing IP address allocation.

steps.title: Add MetalLB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official MetalLB Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add metallb https://metallb.universe.tf

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the MetalLB repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install MetalLB CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required by MetalLB.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metallb/metallb/v0.15.3/manifests/namespace.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metallb/metallb/v0.15.3/manifests/metallb.yaml

```

> Creates the MetalLB namespace.
> Installs the MetalLB components.

steps.title: Install MetalLB using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs MetalLB in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --version 0.15.3

```

> Installs MetalLB with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a MetalLB ConfigMap
steps.description:
This step creates a ConfigMap to define the IP address pool for MetalLB.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    address-pools:
    - name: default
      protocol: layer2
      addresses:
      - 192.168.1.200-192.168.1.250
EOF

```

> Replace the IP range with your actual available IPs.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system

data:
  config: |

    - name: default

EOF

steps.title: Verify MetalLB installation
steps.description:
This step checks if MetalLB components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n metallb-system

kubectl get svc -n metallb-system

```

> Ensure all MetalLB pods are in 'Running' state.
> Check if the MetalLB service is available.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MetalLB
uninstall.description:
This step removes MetalLB and its associated resources. Be aware that this will remove any load balancer services using MetalLB, which may lead to data loss if not handled properly.

```bash

helm uninstall metallb --namespace metallb-system

kubectl delete namespace metallb-system

```

> Uninstalls MetalLB Helm release.
> Deletes the MetalLB namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MetalLB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to back up your configuration and drain any services using MetalLB. This process includes upgrading the Helm release and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get configmap config -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml

helm upgrade metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --version 0.15.3

kubectl get pods -n metallb-system

```

> Backup current MetalLB configuration.
> Upgrades MetalLB to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get configmap config -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'service not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service you are trying to access is not properly configured or MetalLB is not running.

**Fix:**
Check the MetalLB pods and service configurations.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no available IP addresses'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The configured IP address pool is exhausted or not properly defined.

**Fix:**
Ensure the address pool in the ConfigMap is correctly defined and has available IPs.

troubleshooting.title: MetalLB pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the MetalLB configuration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the MetalLB pods for errors using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n metallb-system'.

troubleshooting.title: Load balancer service is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be correctly configured or the IP address is not in the correct range.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure the assigned IP is within the defined address pool.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for BGP peering, improved layer2 configuration options.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.14, ensure to review the new BGP configuration options.

resolution.summary: You should see MetalLB pods running with 'kubectl get pods -n metallb-system' showing all pods in 'Running' state and services assigned IPs from the defined pool.

data:

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources and tools installed.
